questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The equilibrium constant for the reaction
$\mathrm{SO}_2(g)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_3(g)$ is $5 \times 10^{-2}$ atm.
The equilibrium constant of the reaction
$2 \mathrm{SO}_3(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_2(g)+\mathrm{O}_2(g)$ would be
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} \mathrm{SO}_2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2 & \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_3 \\ K_1 & =\frac{\left[\mathrm{SO}_3\right]}{\left[\mathrm{SO}_2\right]\left[\mathrm{O}_2\right]^{1 / 2}} \\ 2 \mathrm{SO}_3 & \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_2+\mathrm{O}_2 \\ \mathrm{~K}_2 & =\frac{\left[\mathrm{SO}_2\right]^2\left[\mathrm{O}_2\right]}{\left[\mathrm{SO}_3\right]^2}\end{aligned}$
From Eqs. (i) and (ii)
$\begin{aligned} K_2 & =\frac{1}{K_1^2} \\ K_2 & =\frac{1}{\left(5 \times 10^{-2}\right)^2}=\frac{1}{25 \times 10^{-4}} \\ & =\frac{100 \times 10^2}{25} \\ & =4 \times 10^2 \mathrm{~atm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider the following reaction in a $1 \mathrm{~L}$ closed vessel.
$$
\mathrm{N}_2+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_3
$$

If all the species; $\mathrm{N}_2, \mathrm{H}_2$ and $\mathrm{NH}_3$ are in $1 \mathrm{~mol}$ in the beginning of the reaction and equilibrium is attained after unreacted $\mathrm{N}_2$ is $0.7 \mathrm{~mol}$. What is the value of equilibrium constant?
questions.solution:
For the given reaction:
$$
\mathrm{N}_2+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_3
$$
$\begin{array}{llll}\text { Initial moles } & 1 & 1 & 1\end{array}$
At equilibrium $1-x \quad 1-3 x \quad 2 x$
Given, $1-x=0.7 \mathrm{~mol}$
$\therefore \quad x=0.3 \mathrm{~mol}$
Therefore, concentration of $\mathrm{N}_2, \mathrm{H}_2$ and $\mathrm{NH}_3$ at equilibrium will be
$$
\begin{aligned}
{\left[\mathrm{N}_2\right] } & =[0.7] \\
{\left[\mathrm{H}_2\right] } & =1-(3 \times 0.3)=[0.1] \\
{\left[\mathrm{NH}_3\right] } & =1+2 x=1+(2 \times 0.3)=[1.6]
\end{aligned}
$$

According to law of equilibrium constant $\left(K_C\right)$
$$
\begin{aligned}
K_C & =\frac{\left[\mathrm{NH}_3\right]^2}{\left[\mathrm{~N}^2\right],\left[\mathrm{H}_2\right]^3}=\frac{[\mathrm{l} .6]^2}{[0.7][0.1]^3} \\
K_C & =\frac{(256)}{0.007}=3657.14
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence, equilibrium constant $\left(K_C\right)=3657.14$ and (b) is the correct option.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider the following reaction equilibrium:
$\mathrm{N}_2(g)+3 \mathrm{H}_2(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_3(g)$
Initially, 1 mole of $\mathrm{N}_2$ and 3 moles of $\mathrm{H}_2$ are taken in a $2 \mathrm{~L}$ flask. At equilibrium state if, the number of moles of $\mathrm{N}_2$ is 0.6 , what is the total number of moles of all gases present in the flask?
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{N}_2(g)+3 \mathrm{H}_2(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_3(g)$
$\begin{array}{ll}1 \text { mole } 3 \text { mole } & 0 \text { moles (2 L) (initially) } \\ 0.6 \text { or } \quad(3-3 x) & 2 x \text { moles } \\ (1-x) \text { moles moles } & \text { (at equilibrium) }\end{array}$
Number of moles of $\mathrm{N}_2=0.6=1-x$
$\therefore x=1-0.6=0.4$
So, $3-3 x=3-3 \times 0.4=1.8$,
and $2 x=2 \times 0.4=0.8$
Therefore, the total number of moles at equilibrium.
$=(1+x)+(3-3 x)+2 x$
$=0.6+1.8+0.8=3.2 \mathrm{~mol}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For the reaction <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced></msub><mo>⇌</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced></msub><mo>+</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>33</mn><mo>%</mo></math> dissociated at a total pressure <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The correct relation between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></msub></math> is
questions.solution:
Given,

Total pressure=P

Degree of dissociation =33% = 0.33 

The given equilibrium reaction is,

A(g)⇌B(g)+C(g)at t=0100at t=Teq1-ααα

Now, Total number of moles = 1-α+α+α =1+α 

Partial pressure of A =1-α1+α×P

Partial pressure of B =α1+α×P

Partial pressure of C =α1+α×P

The relation between the KP and the total pressure is,

KP =PB×PCPA

Putting all the values of partial pressure, we get

KP =α2P1-α2

KP=0.332P1-0.332

KP =0.122P

P =8.19KP

P≈8KP

Thus, the relation between P and KP is P =8KP
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: At $T(K), K_C$ value for the reaction $\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~N}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \frac{2}{3} \mathrm{NH}_3(\mathrm{~g})$ is 50 . The $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{C}}$ value for the reaction $2 \mathrm{NH}_3(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{N}_2(\mathrm{~g})+3 \mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ at the same temperature is
questions.solution:
$\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~N}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \rightleftharpoons \frac{2}{3} \mathrm{NH}_3 \mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{C}}=50$ given.
Multiply by 3 in eq ${ }^{\text {n }}$
$$
\therefore \quad \mathrm{N}_2+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_3 \text {. }
$$
Reverse eq (2)
$$
\therefore \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{C}}^{\prime}=(50)^3
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \quad 2 \mathrm{NH}_3 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{N}_2+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{C}^{\prime}}=\left(\frac{1}{50}\right)^3 \\
& \therefore \quad \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{C}^{\prime}}=\frac{1}{50} \times \frac{1}{50} \times \frac{1}{50}=8 \times 10^{-6} .
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
For the reaction:      2 NO 2 g ⇌ 2 NO g + O 2 g ,     KC = 1.8×10-6 at 184oC, R = 0.0831 kJ K-1 mol-1
when, KP and KC are compared at 184oC it is found that:
options: KP is greater  thanKC
Whether KP is greater than, less than or equal to KCdepends upon the total gas pressure.
questions.solution:
The realation between different equilibrium constant is given as:

KP= KC(RT)ΔngΔng=∑gaseous moles of products-∑gaseousmolesofreactentsΔng=2+1-2=1KP=KC(RT)KP>KC
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In which of the following plots, an endothermic reaction is correctly represented?
questions.solution:
$K_p$ and $T$ are related as follows :
$K_p=K_c \cdot(R T)^{\Delta z_g}$
where,
$\begin{aligned} & K_p=\text { Equilibrium constant in pressure terms } \\ & K_c=\text { Equilibrium constant in concentration terms. } \\ & \begin{aligned} R & =\text { Gas constant } \\ T & =\text { Temperature } \\ \Delta n_g & =\text { Gaseous moles of products } \\ & \quad-\text { gaseous moles of reactants }\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$
Therefore,
$K_p \propto T \quad$ (for another reaction)
Thus, the plot between $\frac{1}{T}$ and $\ln K_F$ shows a downward straight line slope for $\ln K_p$. Hence, option (d) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: At $400 \mathrm{~K}$, in a $1.0 \mathrm{~L}$ vessel, $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4$ is allowed to attain equilibrium, $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ At equilibrium, the total pressure is $600 \mathrm{~mm} \mathrm{Hg}$, when $20 \%$ of $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4$ is dissociated. The value of $K_n$ for the reaction is
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{lcc}\text { } & \mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4 \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_2 \\ \text { Initial moles } & 1 & 0 \\ \text { At equilibrium } & (1-0.2) & 2 \times 0.2=0.8 \\ \text { Total moles }=0.4+0.8=12\end{array}$
$K_P=\frac{\left(P_{\mathrm{NO}_2}\right)^2}{\left(P_{\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4}\right)} \Rightarrow K_P=\frac{\left(\frac{0.4}{1.2} \times 600\right)^2}{\left(\frac{0.8}{1.2} \times 600\right)}=100$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: At \(1000 \mathrm{~K}\), the partial pressures of \(\mathrm{CO}_2(g)\) and \(\mathrm{CO}(g)\) for the reaction \(\mathrm{CO}_2(g)+\mathrm{C}(s) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{CO}(g)\) in a closed vessel at equilibrium are 0.15 and 0.60 bar respectively. The \(K_c\) for this reaction at the same temperature is approximately
questions.solution:
For the reaction,
\(\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{CO}_2(g)+\mathrm{C}(s) & \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{CO}(g) \\
K_{\mathrm{sp}}= & \frac{\left[\mathrm{CO}^2(g)\right.}{\left[\mathrm{CO}_2\right](g)}=\frac{p\left[\mathrm{CO}^2(g)\right.}{p\left[\mathrm{CO}_2\right](g)} \\
& {\left[\therefore p V=n R T \text { or } \frac{n}{V}=\frac{p}{R T}, \frac{n}{V} \propto p(R, T)\right] }
\end{aligned}\)
Given,
\(\begin{aligned}
p[\mathrm{CO}] & =0.6 \text { bar } \\
p\left[\mathrm{CO}_2\right] & =0.15 \text { bar } \\
K_p & =\frac{[0.6]^2}{[0.15]}=2.4
\end{aligned}\)
Also, \(K_p=K_c \cdot(R T)^{\Delta n}\)
where, \(\Delta n=\) moles of gaseous products moles of gaseous reactants \(=2-1=1\)
\(\therefore \quad K_p=K_c \times R \times T\)
where,
\(\begin{aligned}
K_c & =\frac{K_p}{R T} \\
K_p & =2.4 \\
R & =0.082 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~atm} \mathrm{~K}
\end{aligned}\)
or \(K_c=2.89 \times 10^{-2}\)
Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The supply of oxygen to tissues by blood, can be explained by
questions.solution:
Le-Chatelier's principle drives the following reaction forward as blood passes through the alveoli
\(\mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{HCO}_3 \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3 \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\)
The carbon dioxide produced here continually diffuses into the alveoli and is exhaled, ensuring favorable kinetics for the reaction to proceed. Thus, correct answer is option (a).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is not a characteristic property of chemical equilibrium ?
options: Rate of forward reaction is equal to rate of backward reaction at equilibrium.
After reaching the chemical equilibrium, the concentrations of reactants and products remain unchanged with time.
For $A(g) \rightleftharpoons B(g), K_c$ is $10^{-2}$. If this reaction is carried out in the presence of catalyst, the value of $K_c$ decreases
After reaching the equilibrium, both forward and backward reactions continue to take place
questions.solution: There will be no effect on equilibrium constant $\left(K_c\right)$ in presence of catalyst. So, statement (c) is wrong.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Ammonium sulphide and ammonium selenide on heating dissociates as

(NH4)2S(s) ⇌ 2NH3(g)+ H2S(g); Kp1 = 9 × 10-3 atm3

(NH4)2Se(s) ⇌ 2NH3(g) + H2Se(g); Kp2 = 4.5 × 10-3 atm3

The total pressure over the solid mixture at equilibrium is
questions.solution:
NH 4 2 S s ⇌ 2NH 3 g + H 2 S g                       2p              p

NH 4 2 Se s ⇌ 2NH 3 g + H 2 Se  g                       2q              q

K p 1 = p NH 3 2 · p H 2 S = 2 p + 2 q 2 · p = 9.0 × 10 - 3

K p 2 = p NH 3 2 · p H 2 S e = 2 p + 2 q 2 · q = 4.5 × 10 - 3

∴ p q = 2 ⇒ p = 2 q

∴ K p 1 = 2p + p 2 · p = 9.0 × 10 - 3

∴ p = 0.1 atm and q = 0.1 2 = 0.05

Total pressure = 2p + 2q + p + q = 3p + 3q

                        = 3 × 0.1 + 3 × 0.05 = 0.45 atm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion (A) : For an endothermic dissolution process, increase in temperature increases the solubility in a nearly saturated solution.
Reason (R) : In saturated solution dynamic equilibrium exists between dissolved solute and undissolved solute.
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct and R is the correct explanation of $\mathrm{A}$
Both (A) and (R) are correct, but R is not the correct explanation of $\mathrm{A}$
(A) is correct but (R) is not correct
questions.solution: Increase in temperature in an endothermic resolution process enhance the kinetic energy of the solvent molecules by allowing them to break apart more efficiently. This results into the more solubility of solute molecules.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Dissociation constant of propionic acid is $1.32 \times 10^{-5}$. Calculate the degree of dissociation of acid in $0.05 \mathrm{M}$ solution.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: During the mixing of 4 moles each of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> gases are allowed to react to form <math><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> in a closed vessel. At equilibrium 25% of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is used up. Calculate the  total number of moles of all the gases at equilibrium is-
questions.solution:
2S O 2 initial moles            4 + O 2 4 ⇌ 2S O 3 0

at equilibrium 4−2n      4−n     2n

∴x= 25 100 =.25

At equilibrium = 4(1-0.5), 4(1-0.25), 0.25×2

Total number of moles at equilibrium

=4(1-0.5)+4(1-0.25)+0.5
=4-2+4-1+0.5
=5.5
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The dissociation equilibrium of a gas AB 2 can be represented as,
2  AB 2 (g)⇌2 AB(g)+ B 2 (g)
The degree of dissociation is x and is small as compared to 1 . The expression relating the degree of dissociation (x) with equilibrium constant K P and total pressure P is:
questions.solution:
2 AB⁡ 2 g ⇌ 2AB⁡ g + B⁡ 2 g⁡
At equilibrium   2(1−x)           2x           x
Total moles = (2 + x)
Partial pressures
P AB 2 = 2(1−x) 2 + x p,  P AB  =  2x 2 + x p,  P B 2 = x 2+x p
K p = p AB 2 pB 2 ( pAB 2 ) 2 = [ ( 2 x 2 + x ) p ] 2 [ x p 2 + x ] [ 2 ( 1 - x ) 2 + x p ] 2
K p = 4 x 3 P 4 2+x (1−x) 2
2+x →2 ( x is small)
1−x →1
K P = 4 x 3 p 8 = x 3 p 2
x= 2kp p 3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: When N<sub>2</sub>O<sub>5</sub> is heated at certain temperature, it dissociates as <math> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>N</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <msub> <mtext>O</mtext> <mn>5</mn> </msub> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>g</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mo>⇌</mo> <msub> <mtext>N</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <msub> <mtext>O</mtext> <mn>3</mn> </msub> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>g</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mo>+</mo> <msub> <mtext>O</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>g</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <msub> <mo>; K</mo> <mtext>c</mtext> </msub> <mo>=</mo> <mtext>2.5</mtext> </mrow> </math>. At the same time N<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub> also decomposes as : <math> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>N</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <msub> <mtext>O</mtext> <mn>3</mn> </msub> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>g</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mo>⇌</mo> <msub> <mtext>N</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <mtext>O</mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>g</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> <mo>+</mo> <msub> <mtext>O</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>g</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </math>. If initially 4.0 moles of N<sub>2</sub>O<sub>5</sub> are taken in 1.0 litre flask and allowed to dissociation, concentration of O<sub>2</sub> at equilibrium is 2.5 M. Equilibrium concentration of N<sub>2</sub>O<sub>5</sub> is :
questions.solution:
N 2 O 5 ⇌ N 2 O 3 + O 2 4 - x x - y x + y N 2 O 3 ⇌ N 2 O + O 2 x - y y y + x

∵   O 2 = x + y = 2.5

and   2.5 = x + y x - y 4 - x

for 1st reaction    Kc= 2.5 = x - 2.5 + x × 2.5 4 - x = 2 x - 2.5 4 - x 2.5

4 - x = 2 x - 2.5

6.5 = 3 x   ∴   x = 2.166

N 2 O 5 = 4 - x = 4 - 2.166 = 1.834 M
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In the relation, <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></mrow></msub><msup><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></mrow></msup></math> the value of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></math> is:
options: Number of moles of gaseous reactants <math><mo>-</mo></math> number of moles of gaseous products in a balanced equation
Number of moles of gaseous products <math><mo>-</mo></math> number of moles of gaseous reactants in a balanced equation
Number of moles of gaseous products <math><mo>×</mo></math> number of moles of gaseous reactants in a balanced equation
Number of moles of gaseous reactants <math><mo>+</mo></math> number of moles of gaseous products in a balanced equation
questions.solution:
In the relation, Kp=Kc(RT)Δn, the term ∆n refers to the difference in the total number of moles of gaseous products and the total number of moles of gaseous reactants. Hence, it can have either positive value or negative value. It can be zero or also an integer or a fraction.

Δng=ng(p)-ng(r)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub></math> is dissociated to 33% and 40% at total pressure <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> atm respectively. Then the ratio <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>/</mo><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is
questions.solution:
KP=(nNO2)2nN2O4×PΣn1
For 33% dissociation KP=(2×0.33)20.67×P1.33
For 40% dissociation KP=(2×0.40)20.60×P1.40
So P1P2=1.56≃1.60=85 .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q:
The equilibrium constant for the reaction   SO 3 g ⇌ SO 2 g + 1 2 O 2 g        is Kc=4·9×10-2.

The value of KC for the reaction 2 SO 2 g + O 2 g ⇌ 2 SO 3 g  will be -
questions.solution:
SO 3 g ⇌ SO 2 g + 1 / 2 O 2 g

SO2O21/2SO3=Kc=4·9×10-2

SO2g+12O2g⇌SO3g

SO3SO3O21/2=Kc'=14·9×10-2

For    2SO2g+O2g⇌2SO3g

SO32SO22O2=Kc'2=14·9×4·9×10-4

                              = 1 0 0 0 0 2 4 · 0 1 = 4 1 6 · 4 9

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: When sulphur in the form of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msub></math> is heated at 900 K the initial pressure of 1 atm falls by 29% at equilibrium. This is because of conversion of some <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msub></math> to <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> . Find the value of equilibrium constant for this reaction.
questions.solution:
S8(g)11-x⇌4S2(g)04x
x=29%=0.29
Kp=(PS2)4(PS8)=(4x)2(1-x) atm3
=(4×0.29)41-0.29=2.55 atm3 .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q:
The vapour pressure of mercury is 0.002 mm Hg at 27°C
Hg(l)⇌Hg(g)
The value of equilibrium constant KC is
questions.solution:
Hg(l)⇌Hg(g)
Δng=1
Kp=0.002760=2.63×10-6 atm
Kp=Kc(RT)Δn
Kc=KpRT=2.63×10-60.0821×300
=1.068×10-7 M

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
When N2O5 is heated at certain temperature, it dissociates as

           N 2 O 5 g ⇌ N 2 O 3 g + O 2 g , K c = 2.5

at the same time N2O3 also decomposes as N 2 O 3 g ⇌ N 2 O g + O 2 g
In initially 4 mol of N2O5 are taken in 1 L flask and allowed to dissociate, concentration of O2 at equilibrium is 2.5 M, equilibrium concentration of N2O3 will be
questions.solution:
Solution writing equilibrium concentrations of simultaneous equilibria (V=1 Litre)

N 2 O 5 4 - x ⇌ N 2 O 3 g x - y + O 2 g x + y ; K c = 2.5

N 2 O 3 x - y ⇌ N 2 O y + O 2 x + y

O 2 = x + y = 2.5

K c = 2.5 = N 2 O 3 O 2 N 2 O 5 = x - y x + y 4 - x

as y = 2.5 - x ; Substituting

( x - 2.5 + x ) 2.5 4 - x = 2.5

2 x - 2.5 = 4 - x

∴ 3 x = 6.5

∴ x = 2.17

    y = 0.33

N 2 O 3 = x - y = 2.17 - 0.33

                x - y = 1.84 = N 2 O 3

Choice 1.8
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
At a certain temperature the equilibrium constant KC is 0.25 for the reaction
A(g)+B(g)⇌C(g)+D(g)
If we take 1 mole of each of four gases in a 10 litre container, what would be the equilibrium concentration of A(g)?
questions.solution:
The give reaction will go in backward direction

Kc=(A)×(B)(C)×(D)
4=(1+α)2(1-α)2
2=1+α1-α
2-2α=1+α
α=13=0.33
[A]=1+α10
=1+0.3310=0.133 M
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q:
0.020 g of selenium vapour at equilibrium occupying a volume of 2.463 mL at 1 atm and 27oC. The selenium is in a state of equilibrium according to reaction

3 Se 2 g ⇌ Se 6 g

What is the degree of association of selenium ?

(Atomic weight of Se = 79)
questions.solution:
3 Se 2 g ⇌ Se 6 g At equilibrium a 1 - α a α 3

Total moles at equilibrium = a 1 - 2 α 3

a 1 - 2 α 3 = n Total = PV RT

= 1 atm × 2.463 × 1 0 - 3 0.0821 × 3 0 0 = 1 0 - 4

a = 0.020 g 79 × 2 g / mole (Initial moles)

   = 0.020 1 5 8 = 1.266 × 1 0 - 4

1.266 × 1 0 - 4 1 - 2 α 3 = 1 0 - 4

1 - 2 α 3 = 1 1.266 = 0.79

2 α 3 = 0.21 ⇒ α = 0.63 2 = 0.315
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q:
The equilibrium constant (Kp) for the decomposition of gaseous H2O

H2Og⇌H2g+12O2(g)

is related to degree of dissociation (α) at a total pressure p is given by
questions.solution:
H2Og⇌H2g+12O2(g)  1             0        0     (initally)1-α          α        α2   (at equilibrium)   


Total moles of water at equilibrium = 1-α+α+α2=1-α+α+α2=1+α2

Partial pressure of H2O=partial pressure*molefraction=p*no of moles of watertotal number of moles=p*1-α1+α2=p2(1-α)2+α

Partial pressure of H2=partial pressure*molefraction=p*no of moles of Htotal number of moles=α1+α2

Partial pressure of O2=partial pressure*molefraction=p*no: of moles of oxygentotal number of moles=pα2+α

Kp=partial pressures of pdtspartial pressure of reactants=pH2.pO21/2pH2O=pα1+α2*pα2+α12p*2-2α2+α=p2α2+αpα2+α12p*2-2α2+α=α*α12p12(2+α)12(1-α)=p12α32(2+α)12(1-α)

Kp=α3/2p1/21-α2+α1/2

questions.q:
Kp for the reaction

CuSO 4 · 3 H 2 O s + 2 H 2 O g → CuSO 4 · 5 H 2 O s    is 1 · 0 × 1 0 4 atm - 2   at certain  temperature. What lowest relative humidity of air can be achieved using CuS O 4 .3 H 2 O as drying agent at that temperature ?
Aqueous tension at the given temperature = 16 torr
questions.solution:
CuSO 4 · 3 H 2 O s + 2 H 2 O g ⇌ CuSO 4 · 5 H 2 O g

K p = 1 p H 2 O g 2 = 1 × 1 0 4 atm - 2

p H 2 O g = 1 × 1 0 - 2 atm = 7 · 6  mm of Hg

Relative Humidity of Air in Environment = 7 · 6  m m 1 6  m m

= 4 7 · 5 %
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The effect of temperature on the equilibrium constant is expressed as, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>></mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mo> </mo><msub><mi>logK</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>/</mo><msub><mi>logK</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>ΔH</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>303</mn></mrow></mfrac><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>. For endothermic reactions, false statement is:
questions.solution:
Given that:

logK2K1=-ΔH2.3031 T2-1 T1.

For endothermic reactions, ∆H>0.

Now, for T2>T1:

⇒logK2K1=-ΔH2.303T2-T1T1T2⇒logK2K1>0⇒log K2-log K1>0⇒log K2>log K1∴K2>K1.

So, 1T2-1T1<0.     (∴T2>T1)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The decomposition of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>HNO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> represented below attains equilibrium at a given <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi>eq</mi></msub></math><sub>.</sub> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math>. If we start <math> <mrow> <mn>4</mn> <msub> <mtext>HNO</mtext> <mrow> <mn>3</mn> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mi>c</mi> <mtext>g</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </msub> <mo>⇌</mo> <mn>4</mn> <msub> <mtext>NO</mtext> <mrow> <mn>2</mn> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>g</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </msub> <mo>+</mo> <mn>2</mn> <msub> <mtext>H</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <msub> <mtext>O</mtext> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>g</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </msub> <mo>+</mo> <msub> <mtext>O</mtext> <mrow> <mn>2</mn> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>g</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </msub> </mrow> </math> with pure <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>HNO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> having initial pressure <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi></msub></math><sub>, </sub> then at equilibrium which among the following is correct?
options: <math><mrow>  <msub>          <mtext>K</mtext>        <mtext>P</mtext>  </msub>  <mo>=</mo>  <mfrac>    <mrow>      <mn>1</mn>      <mn>0</mn>      <mn>2</mn>      <mn>4</mn>      <msup>            <mrow>              <mfenced open="(" close=")" separators=",">                <mrow>                  <msub>                          <mtext>Po</mtext>                        <mn>2</mn>                  </msub>                </mrow>              </mfenced>            </mrow>            <mn>7</mn>      </msup>    </mrow>    <mrow>      <msup>            <mrow>              <mfenced open="(" close=")" separators=",">                <mrow>                  <msub>                          <mtext>P</mtext>                        <mtext>eq.</mtext>                  </msub>                  <mo>-</mo>                  <mn>7</mn>                  <msub>                          <mtext>Po</mtext>                        <mn>2</mn>                  </msub>                </mrow>              </mfenced>            </mrow>            <mn>4</mn>      </msup>    </mrow>  </mfrac></mrow></math>

<math> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>K</mtext> <mtext>P</mtext> </msub> <mo>=</mo> <mfrac> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> <mn>0</mn> <mn>2</mn> <mn>4</mn> <msup> <mrow> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>Po</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> <mn>7</mn> </msup> </mrow> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>P</mtext> <mtext>i</mtext> </msub> <mo>-</mo> <mn>4</mn> <msub> <mtext>Po</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> <mn>4</mn> </msup> </mrow> </mfrac> </mrow> </math>

<math><mrow>  <msubsup>          <mtext>K</mtext>        <mtext>P</mtext>        <mo>∘</mo>  </msubsup>  <mo>=</mo>  <mfrac>    <mrow>      <mn>1</mn>      <mn>0</mn>      <mn>2</mn>      <mn>4</mn>      <msup>            <mrow>              <mfenced open="(" close=")" separators=",">                <mrow>                  <msub>                          <mtext>Po</mtext>                        <mn>2</mn>                  </msub>                </mrow>              </mfenced>            </mrow>            <mn>7</mn>      </msup>    </mrow>    <mrow>      <msup>            <mrow>              <mfenced open="(" close=")" separators=",">                <mrow>                  <msub>                          <mtext>P</mtext>                        <mtext>eq.</mtext>                  </msub>                  <mo>-</mo>                  <mn>7</mn>                  <msub>                          <mtext>Po</mtext>                        <mn>2</mn>                  </msub>                </mrow>              </mfenced>            </mrow>            <mn>4</mn>      </msup>    </mrow>  </mfrac>  <mo>×</mo>  <msup>        <mrow>          <mfenced open="(" close=")" separators=",">            <mrow>              <mfrac>                <mrow>                  <mn>1</mn>                </mrow>                <mrow>                  <msup>                          <mtext>P</mtext>                        <mo>∘</mo>                  </msup>                </mrow>              </mfrac>            </mrow>          </mfenced>        </mrow>        <mn>3</mn>  </msup></mrow></math>
questions.solution:
4HNO3g⇌4NO2g+2H2Og+O2;Pressure at equilibrium is Peq.Pi000Pi-4P4P2PP

P eq. = P i - 4 P + 4 P + 2 P + P

∴   P i - 4 P = P eq. - 7 P = P eq. - 7 Po 2 ; P = Po 2

K P = P NO 2 4 P H 2 O 2 Po 2 P HNO 3 4

     = 1 0 2 4 Po 2 7 P eq. - 7 Po 2 4 = 1 0 2 4 Po 2 7 P i - 4 Po 2 4

and   K P ∘ = P NO 2 4 P H 2 O 2 Po 2 P HNO 3 4 × 1 P 0 3

              = 4 P 2 1 × 2 P 2 × P P eq. - 7 Po 2 4 × 1 P 0 3

              = 1 0 2 4 Po 2 7 P eq. - 7 Po 2 4 = 1 P 0 3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: For the reaction, <math><mn>2</mn><mi>A</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>g</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>⇌</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>C</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>g</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>D</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>s</mi><mo>)</mo></math>, the value of <math><msub><mrow><mi>K</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>c</mi></mrow></msub></math> will be equal to
questions.solution:
2Ag⇌3Cg+D(s)

For this reaction, ∆ng=3-2=1

∴ Kp=KcRT1

or Kc=KpRT
